commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement Extended Link Opaque LSA parser and database detail display

- Parse Extended Link TLV (RFC 7684 §3) with Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §5)
  and LAN Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §6) Sub-TLVs
- Add show database detail output for Extended Link LSAs
- Clean up remaining debug println statements in ifsm.rs and inst.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add create task dialog, auto-save, multi-file drop, and comments/data fixes

- Add CreateTaskDialog: when filters are active, Enter opens a dialog instead
  of inserting an inline block, supporting #hashtag and @mention parsing
- Add debounced auto-save (3s default) for inline note title edits
- Support multi-file drag & drop in BlockNoteEditor with parallel uploads,
  and inject delete buttons on file blocks
- Simplify comments UI: remove reply/resolve threading, flatten to a linear
  comment list with compact layout and toast on save
- Make comments fully optimistic: instant UI updates with pending-mutation
  guard to prevent realtime subscription from overwriting local state
- Fix foreign key violations: await note insert in Supabase before emitting
  label/assignee events in createNote and createNoteAfter
- Add optimistic assignee cache updates via event subscription
- Change default note sort from sort_order to created_at descending
- Fix GCal calendar fetch deduplication with one-shot ref
- Add is_default field to Contact type, fix Supabase type casts
- Remove note_comments updated_at DB trigger (managed in app code)
- Add i18n keys for create task dialog and comment saved toast (en/es/pt)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

with objectContaining to avoid brittleness from additional default headers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement OSPF SR TLV parsing and database detail display

- Move SidLabelTlv to packet-utils crate for sharing between isis-packet and ospf-packet
- Add OSPF-style SID/Label Sub-TLV parser (2+2 byte header vs IS-IS 1+1)
- Parse RouterInfo TLVs: SID/Label Range, Local Block, Router Capability, Algorithm
- Implement Extended Prefix Opaque LSA parser (RFC 7684) with Prefix SID Sub-TLV (RFC 8665)
- Add "show ip ospf database detail" output for Opaque Area LSAs with SR details
- Clean up debug println statements in nfsm.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pi-hole DNS toggle: WebSocket streaming with step-by-step status

Replaced the simple HTTP toggle with a WebSocket endpoint that streams
each step: saving config, looking up Pi-hole IP, generating compose,
restarting exit node (with docker compose output), and verifying the
container is running (with auto-retry if it fails).

Frontend shows a live log panel with colored status lines during the
operation. This replaces the blind "Enabling..."/"Disabling..." spinner.

commit.message:
RAG: reranker score gate to prevent irrelevant chunk injection

Add min_score=-2.0 threshold to reranker.rerank() using bge-reranker-base
raw logit scale (clearly irrelevant ~-10 to -3, relevant is positive).
Chunks that don't clear the threshold are dropped; if nothing survives,
get_document_context() returns "" and skips injection entirely.

Fixes the case where the retrieval stage (wide distance net) pulls in
document chunks that are semantically far from the query — the reranker
now acts as an accurate quality gate, not just a sorter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: automate driver code normalization in ingestion pipeline

- Add upfront driver code normalization using OpenF1 mapping before
  storing session classifications (prevents unique constraint violations)
- Add _fix_numeric_driver_codes() as safety net post-ingestion
- Add fix-driver-codes CLI command for manual fixing of existing data
- Add DriverCodeFixResult to distinguish mapping failures from no-ops
- Use proper cache invalidation helpers instead of manual Redis patterns

The ingestion pipeline now automatically converts numeric driver codes
(e.g., "63") to proper abbreviations (e.g., "RUS") using OpenF1 data,
eliminating the need for manual script intervention after each session.

commit.message:
Fix invisible date badge on image cells using TDD User reported: "I can't see the date numbers on calendar cells that have pictures. Please use test driven development to write a test for this case and then make it pass." Root cause: the badge was a sibling of the inside the same overflow/contain clipping context. In some browsers (particularly Safari with contain:paint), the clipping suppressed the badge. Fix: wrap the image in its own dedicated
clipping container. The badge is now a sibling of that wrapper (not inside it), so no clipping context can ever hide it. Also removed contain:paint from the Link — the image wrapper handles its own clipping. TDD: wrote failing test "badge is NOT inside the image clipping container" first, confirmed it failed, then implemented the fix to make it pass. Updated related containment tests to reflect the new structure (22 total). Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

Major rebalance, new content, UI overhaul, and QoL features

Zone prices significantly increased, fish sell values reduced ~25%, net
upgrades made exponentially expensive with smaller initial radius. Luck
boost nerfed. Sand Dunes now 100k entry. Fish bleed extended to 2 zones
with distance penalty. Added clickable creatures to all zones (reef urchin,
mine gem, loose tooth, city relic, wall shard). New jellyfish and angler
fish shapes for deep zones. Fish speed scales with depth, epic/legendary
multipliers increased. Reef decor anchored to floor, sand dunes background
made more dramatic. Golden fish now show gold aura only (keep rarity colors).
Navbar restructured: currency floats above center, consumables bottom-left,
nets bottom-right, export/import moved to top-right HUD. Ability buttons
enlarged with clearer cooldown text. Net placement reworked with right-click
removal and 2-min cooldown system. Auto-save to localStorage every 60s.
Game pauses when window is below 80% screen size.

  by value from a helper (copy constructor breaks SSL context)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix knowledge_search tool: correct OpenClaw execute() call signature

The OpenClaw plugin SDK calls execute(_id: string, params: Record<string, unknown>)
with two arguments. Our execute only took one argument and destructured
it as { query }, so it was receiving the tool-call ID string as the
first (and only) arg — query destructured to undefined, which then
threw "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')" inside
retrieve().

Fix:
- tools.ts: update execute to (_id, params) and extract query safely
  with a typeof guard; match the SDK return format
  { content: [{type: "text", text: ...}], details: {...} }
- store.ts: add defensive !query guard before .trim() so retrieve()
  degrades gracefully even if called with a non-string

74 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(mobile): favorites tag navigation shows list instead of detail view (#128)

* fix(mobile): favorites tag navigation shows list instead of detail view

When tapping '♥ Favorites' on mobile, handleTagChange called updateURL
without { replace: true }, triggering a hashchange event that fired
syncStateFromURL. Since tag=__favorites__ has no view param, the mobile
view sync block fell through to the params.toString() !== '' branch and
set mobileView to 'list', overriding the correct 'detail' set moments
earlier.

Two fixes:
1. Add { replace: true } to the updateURL call for __favorites__ in
   handleTagChange, preventing the spurious hashchange.
2. Add an explicit urlTag === '__favorites__' branch in syncStateFromURL's
   mobile view sync block so that direct navigation to #tag=__favorites__
   (e.g., browser bookmark or back/forward) also lands in detail view.

Add two regression tests covering both the click and direct-navigation paths.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01B5iCCL4zEZuoXV6ydjut9r

* fix(mobile): browser back from calendar detail shows homepage instead of list

When pressing the browser back button (or Android hardware back) from a
calendar detail view, the URL reverts to empty and syncStateFromURL treats
it identically to the initial app load — setting mobileView='detail' and
showHomepage=true, which shows the homepage instead of the calendar list.

Fix by passing the event object to syncStateFromURL and checking
event.type === 'popstate'. When a popstate fires on mobile with an empty
URL, switch to 'list' view so the user returns to the calendar list rather
than the homepage. The initial load (no event) and home-button resets
(hashchange) continue to land on the homepage as before.

Add a regression test that simulates navigating to a calendar then
triggering a popstate back to an empty URL, asserting the sidebar
(list view) is shown.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01B5iCCL4zEZuoXV6ydjut9r

* fix(mobile): hashchange after popstate was resetting mobileView to homepage

The previous fix handled popstate correctly but missed that browsers fire
both popstate AND hashchange when the user navigates back through history
(popstate first, hashchange second). The trailing hashchange was calling
syncStateFromURL with event.type='hashchange', skipping the popstate branch
and falling through to the default 'else' case which set mobileView='detail'
(homepage), undoing the fix.

Split the event listeners into separate onPopstate/onHashchange handlers.
onPopstate sets a ref flag and calls syncStateFromURL, then clears the flag
after a setTimeout(0) — enough time for the browser to fire hashchange.
onHashchange skips execution if the flag is set, so the trailing hashchange
from a history navigation doesn't override the popstate result.

Update the regression test to also dispatch hashchange after popstate,
matching real browser behaviour.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01B5iCCL4zEZuoXV6ydjut9r

* feat(mobile): restore calendar list scroll position when navigating back

Save the calendar list scrollTop when the user taps a calendar entry, then
restore it when mobileView switches back to 'list' (in-app back bar or
browser back). Avoids the user being snapped to the top of a long list
after returning from a calendar detail view.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01B5iCCL4zEZuoXV6ydjut9r

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add SL survival models to production (0.3% and 0.5% thresholds)

Both passed experiments with Spearman 0.29/0.26, lift >0.25, all
gens significant. Binary classification (logistic) predicting
P(stop-loss hit within 30m for long positions).

Briefing shows "SL survival (30m): 0.3%: 45% hit risk, 0.5%: 20% hit risk"
with warning when >50%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

EMERGENCY: Dashboard safe mode — 8s hard timeout on all heavy calls

First fix (max_retries=0) deployed but dashboard still 524. Wrap ALL
heavy context (AI insights, engines, data gathering, command brief)
in a single ThreadPoolExecutor with 8s deadline. If anything blocks,
dashboard renders with safe defaults instead of timing out.

commit.message:
feat: discover_multilang + scan_to_graph_json for Java/Go policy discovery

qse-py/src/lib.rs: add scan_to_graph_json() PyO3 binding that returns
internal graph as JSON (nodes with internal flag + edges). Enables
feeding Rust scanner output into Python discover pipeline.

qse/discover.py: add discover_multilang(repo_path) — auto-detects
language via Rust scanner, reconstructs nx.DiGraph, runs discover_policies.
Falls back to Python scanner if Rust not available.

Results:
  guava (Java):       8 clusters, 18 rules (android vs guava boundaries)
  spring-boot (Java): 4 clusters, 4 rules (org.springframework stable core)
  gin (Go):           2 clusters, 2 rules
  vault (Go):         4 clusters (too connected for rules)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix HTML AI enhance stripping styles, literal \n, add markdown->html v3.13.83

Bug 1: HTML enhancement format instructions told the AI to generate
Bootstrap styling from scratch, causing it to rewrite the visual
structure and strip existing classes/colors. Fixed: non-markdown_to_html
HTML enhancements now explicitly preserve all existing CSS classes and
style attributes; only text content is touched.

Bug 2: AI was emitting literal \n strings in HTML output. Added explicit
instruction to use real newline characters, never the backslash-n sequence.

Feature: Added 'Convert Markdown → HTML' enhancement option. Uses the
existing rich Bootstrap styling instructions (cards, badges, alerts,
Font Awesome icons) since this is a fresh conversion, not a preserve job.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: 14 backend hardening improvements — security, robustness, observability, intelligence, testing

Security (FASE 1):
- Centralized cron/admin auth via validateCronOrAdmin with CRON_SECRET (8 routes)
- Server-side Descope JWT verification instead of trusting headers
- SSRF defense with DNS resolution validation in image-proxy
- Admin session TTL enforcement (7-day expiry with auto-cleanup)

Robustness (FASE 2):
- Centralized safeNumber/safeInt helpers replacing fragile Number() parsing
- Zod-based env validation at startup (fail-fast on missing vars)
- Atomic claim in curation queue (UPDATE...RETURNING replaces race-prone fetch+update)
- Public /api/health endpoint (DB, Redis, Worker heartbeat checks)
- Configurable PG connection pool sizing via env vars

Observability (FASE 3):
- Structured logging with Pino in catalog refresh pipeline

Intelligence (FASE 4):
- Auto-pause brands after consecutive refresh failures
- Redis cache layer for catalog facets (RC-4, TTL 15min)
- Smart per-brand refresh scheduling based on change rate (2/7/14 day intervals)

Testing (FASE 5):
- Vitest setup with 27 unit tests (safeNumber, auth, health endpoint)
- CI workflow updated: Node 22, test step, conditional smoke job

so Voice Over step has access to the scenes.

so patients see the real name in messages and push notifications.

fix: switch back to Docker, COPY entire backend/ to /app/backend

Nixpacks was unreliable (apt pip paths, no logs on crash). Docker with
WORKDIR=/app/backend + COPY backend/ . makes ALL modules accessible
(llm_benchmark, agent_swarm, swarm, etc.) without needing individual COPY
steps. No torch/numpy – requirements.txt is minimal and fast.

commit.message:
Add Quinn web chat UI embedded in QAForge

Implements a full-featured AI chat assistant (Quinn) directly inside the
QAForge web UI as a slide-out panel, so users can interact with her
without any external tool (CLI or Claude Desktop).

Phase 1 - Streaming Chat:
- DB migration for chat_sessions + chat_messages tables
- SSE-streamed responses via POST endpoint
- System prompt with live project context (stats, counts, pass rate)
- React chat panel with FAB button, markdown rendering, session mgmt
- Keyboard shortcuts: Cmd+K toggle, Escape close

Phase 2 - Tool Calling:
- 9 tools: project summary, list/get test cases, test plans,
  requirements, KB stats/search, execution results
- LLM orchestrator loop (up to 5 iterations) with tool execution
- complete_with_tools() added to Anthropic, OpenAI, and Groq providers
- Rich tool result cards in the frontend (stat cards, TC tables, bars)

Phase 3 - Polish:
- Session history sidebar with new/delete/select
- Mobile responsive (full-screen overlay)
- Typing indicator, streaming cursor, error banners

Migrate from inline styles to Tailwind CSS utility classes

Configure Tailwind CSS (tailwind.config.ts, postcss.config.mjs, globals.css)
and convert all 29 component/page files from inline style={{}} objects to
Tailwind className strings. Reduces styling code by ~1,585 lines while
maintaining identical visual output.

- Add Tailwind config, PostCSS config, and globals.css with directives
- Move focus-visible and skip-to-content styles from layout.tsx to globals.css
- Add font-sans to body, eliminating repeated fontFamily declarations
- Replace all <style> tags with Tailwind responsive prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:)
- 5 inline styles intentionally retained for dynamic data-driven colors

* feat(con34): add disco-shark enemy type with rainbow tween

- Add 'disco-shark' to EnemyType union in waves.ts
- Add scale 3.5 to exhaustive Record<EnemyType, number> in Enemy.ts
- Add rainbow tint cycle + scale pulse tween for disco-shark
- Update updateHpBar() to size bar like boss for disco-shark
- Add TDD test file con34-disco-shark-enemy.test.ts

* fix(con34): guard rainbow tween cycle against destroyed enemy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(con34): add DiscoSharkController mini-boss system

State machine (idle → spinning → hatching → shark_active → idle).
Triggers at wave index >= 2 with 20% chance, blocked when boss active
or shark already active. Rewards 180 gold on defeat.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(con34): DiscoSharkController waits for ball spin callback before hatching

* fix(con34): call onUiChanged when disco shark reaches end

Both handleSharkDefeated and handleSharkReachedEnd now notify the UI
after resetting state to idle. Added a test covering the reached-end path.

* feat(con34): wire DiscoSharkController into GameScene

* fix(con34): register enemy_disco-shark texture in TextureGenerator

Adds the missing enemy_disco-shark texture so Phaser no longer renders
a placeholder when a Disco Shark spawns. Uses a hot-magenta shark body
with gold fins, a cyan disco eye and white sparkle highlights.

  header bar, simulated text lines)
- disco-shark animation changed to red flicker tween (PHP error feel)
- wave text color changed to black (#000000)

- showNextQuote() added to DiscoRightBallHandle interface and GameScene

* feat(con34): shark death dive destroys nearest tower + Japanese PHP exception texture

- disco-shark texture redesigned as Japanese PHP exception (cream/washi
  paper background, dark red エ header, navy text lines, red hanko seal)
- Enemy.triggerDeathDive() detaches from path and tweens to target
- Enemy.takeDamage() emits 'damaged' event for HP threshold checks

  initiates death dive; tower is destroyed on impact (TURM VERNICHTET!)

  and destroys the game object with an explosion effect

Add SpeedBoostState interface and optional getSpeedBoostState callback to
CombatControllerOptions. New updateSpeedBoosts() method runs each frame to
apply/remove speed boosts on enemies based on their path progress and active path.

Integrate boosted flag into getMovementMultiplier so applyMovementMultiplier
(called when slow timer expires) returns 2.2 instead of resetting to 1.
applySpeedBoost remains idempotent — boosted guard preserved.

repository.name: codeclaw
repository.description: The best IM-driven remote coding experience. Bridge AI coding agents to any IM.

commit.message:
Fix Google Maps not loading on Netlify

Inline the Maps API key directly in churches.html and church.html script tags, removing the dependency on gitignored js/config.js which was never deployed to Netlify, causing the map to load with key=undefined.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
